topics.content: Use 'Hello' and 'Hi' anytime. Use 'Good morning' before 12 PM. 'Good evening' after 6 PM. Say 'Goodbye' when leaving.
topics.content_uz: 'Hello' va 'Hi' ni istalgan vaqt ishlating. 'Good morning' – 12 dan oldin. 'Good evening' – 18:00 dan keyin. Ketayotganda 'Goodbye' ayting.

questions.question: How do you say 'Salom' in English?
What do you say in the morning?

topics.content: To introduce yourself: say 'My name is ___' or 'I am ___'. Ask 'What is your name?' To be polite, say 'Nice to meet you'.
topics.content_uz: O'zingizni tanishtirish uchun: 'My name is ___' yoki 'I am ___' deb ayting. 'What is your name?' deb so'rang. Xushmuomalalik uchun 'Nice to meet you' deb ayting.

questions.question: What do you ask for someone's name?
questions.question_uz: Biror kishining ismini so'rash uchun nima deysiz?

questions.question: What does 'Nice to meet you' mean?

topics.content: Numbers 1-10: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. Practice counting objects around you.
topics.content_uz: 1-10 raqamlari: bir, ikki, uch, to'rt, besh, olti, yetti, sakkiz, to'qqiz, o'n. Atrofingizdagi narsalarni sanashni mashq qiling.

topics.content: Numbers 11-20: eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, eighteen, nineteen, twenty. Notice 'teen' at the end for 13-19.
topics.content_uz: 11-20 raqamlari: eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, eighteen, nineteen, twenty. 13-19 da oxirida 'teen' qo'shimchasi bor.

topics.content: Family words: mother, father, brother, sister, grandmother, grandfather. Use 'my' before them: my mother, my brother.
topics.content_uz: Oila so'zlari: mother, father, brother, sister, grandmother, grandfather. Ulardan oldin 'my' qo'llang: my mother, my brother.

topics.content: Verbs are action words. 'I eat' means you eat. 'You go' means you go. 'Come here' = kel bu yerga.
topics.content_uz: Fe'llar harakat so'zlari. 'I eat' – men yeyman. 'You go' – sen borasan. 'Come here' – kel bu yerga.

items.example: I go to work by bus.

topics.content: Daily activities: wake up → get dressed → eat breakfast → go to work/study → come home → sleep. Use 'I' before each verb.
topics.content_uz: Kundalik ishlar: wake up → get dressed → eat breakfast → go to work/study → come home → sleep. Har bir fe'ldan oldin 'I' qo'llang.

questions.question: What do you do first in the morning?

topics.content: Colors describe things. Say: 'The ___ is (color)'. Example: The car is red.
topics.content_uz: Ranglar narsalarni tasvirlaydi. 'The ___ is (rang)' deb ayting. Misol: Mashina qizil.

topics.content: Week starts on Monday. Use 'on' before days: on Monday, on Sunday.
topics.content_uz: Hafta dushanbadan boshlanadi. Kunlardan oldin 'on' ishlating: on Monday, on Sunday.

questions.question: What is the first day of the week?

topics.content: 12 months: January to December. Use 'in' before months: in January, in May.
topics.content_uz: 12 oy: Yanvardan Dekabrgacha. Oylardan oldin 'in' ishlating: in January, in May.

items.example: What time is it? - It's 3 o'clock.
I wake up in the morning.
I eat lunch in the afternoon.
I watch TV in the evening.

topics.content: To ask time: 'What time is it?' Answer: 'It's ___ o'clock'. Use 'in the' for parts of day: in the morning, in the evening.
topics.content_uz: Vaqtni so'rash uchun: 'What time is it?' Javob: 'It's ___ o'clock'. Kunning qismlari uchun 'in the' ishlating: in the morning, in the evening.

topics.content: Use 'eat' for food, 'drink' for drinks. Say 'I like ___' to express preference.
topics.content_uz: Ovqat uchun 'eat', ichimlik uchun 'drink' ishlating. Istakni bildirish uchun 'I like ___' deb ayting.

items.example: The book is on the desk.

topics.content: Classroom objects: pen, book, desk, chair, pencil, notebook. Use 'a' before singular nouns: a pen, a book.
topics.content_uz: Sinf jihozlari: pen, book, desk, chair, pencil, notebook. Birlik otlardan oldin 'a' ishlating: a pen, a book.

questions.question: Translate: 'Kitob stol ustida'
questions.question_uz: Tarjimasi: 'The book is on the desk'

topics.content: To say your job: 'I am a ___'. Use 'a' for jobs: a teacher, a doctor. For engineer, say 'an engineer' because 'engineer' starts with a vowel sound.
topics.content_uz: Kasbingizni aytish uchun: 'I am a ___'. Kasblar bilan 'a' ishlating: a teacher, a doctor. 'Engineer' uchun 'an engineer' deymiz, chunki 'e' unlisi bilan boshlanadi.

items.example: I buy milk at the shop.

topics.content: Use 'at' for places: at home, at school. Use 'to' with go: go to school, go to the park.
topics.content_uz: Joylar bilan 'at' ishlating: at home, at school. 'Go' bilan 'to' ishlating: go to school, go to the park.

topics.content: Use 'What' to ask about things. Use 'Where' to ask about place. Structure: What/Where + is + this/that?
topics.content_uz: Narsalar haqida so'rash uchun 'What' ishlating. Joy haqida so'rash uchun 'Where' ishlating. Tuzilish: What/Where + is + this/that?

questions.question: Ask about place: 'Kitob qayerda?'
questions.question_uz: Joy haqida so'rang: 'Where is the book?'

topics.content: Yes/no questions start with 'Do', 'Are', 'Is'. Answer with 'Yes' or 'No'. Example: 'Do you like milk?' → 'Yes, I do' or 'No, I don't'.
topics.content_uz: Ha/yo'q savollari 'Do', 'Are', 'Is' bilan boshlanadi. 'Yes' yoki 'No' bilan javob bering. Misol: 'Do you like milk?' → 'Yes, I do' yoki 'No, I don't'.

questions.question: Answer 'Yes' to 'Do you like apples?'
questions.question_uz: 'Do you like apples?' ga 'Yes' deb javob bering

topics.content: I → for yourself. You → for the person you talk to. He → for a man/boy. She → for a woman/girl. It → for thing/animal.
topics.content_uz: I → o'zingiz uchun. You → gaplashayotgan odam uchun. He → erkak/o'gil bola uchun. She → ayol/qiz bola uchun. It → narsa/hayvon uchun.

topics.content: Simple sentence: Subject + verb + object. Example: I (subject) like (verb) apples (object). Always start with a capital letter, end with a dot.
topics.content_uz: Oddiy gap: Ega + fe'l + to'ldiruvchi. Misol: I (ega) like (fe'l) apples (to'ldiruvchi). Har doim bosh harf bilan boshlang, nuqta bilan tugating.

questions.question: What is the subject in 'I see a dog'?
questions.question_uz: 'I see a dog' da ega qaysi?

topics.content: Use 'like' for positive, 'don't like' for negative. 'Love' is stronger than 'like'. 'Hate' is very strong negative. Ask: 'Do you like ___?'
topics.content_uz: Yoqtirish uchun 'like', yoqtirmaslik uchun 'don't like' ishlating. 'Love' – 'like'dan kuchliroq. 'Hate' – juda kuchli salbiy. So'rang: 'Do you like ___?'

questions.question: How to say 'Men sutni yaxshi ko'raman'

items.example: It is cloudy in the morning.

topics.content: To describe weather: 'It is + weather word'. Example: It is sunny. Ask: 'What is the weather like?'
topics.content_uz: Ob-havoni tasvirlash: 'It is + ob-havo so'zi'. Misol: It is sunny. So'rang: 'What is the weather like?'

topics.content: Use 'wear' with clothes: I wear a shirt. Use 'put on' for action. Use 'take off' for removing.
topics.content_uz: Kiyimlar bilan 'wear' ishlating: I wear a shirt. Harakat uchun 'put on'. Yechish uchun 'take off'.

questions.question: What do you wear on your feet?

items.example: The school is behind the park.
The shop is next to the bank.

topics.content: To give directions: 'Turn left', 'Turn right', 'Go straight'. To describe location: 'behind', 'next to'.
topics.content_uz: Yo'nalish berish uchun: 'Turn left', 'Turn right', 'Go straight'. Joylashuvni tasvirlash: 'behind', 'next to'.

questions.question: Translate: 'Do'kon bankning yonida'
questions.question_uz: Tarjimasi: 'The shop is next to the bank'
options: The shop is behind the bank
The shop is next to the bank
The shop is left of the bank
The shop is right of the bank
questions.answer: The shop is next to the bank

topics.content: Ask price: 'How much is it?' Say 'I want to buy ___'. To say it's too much: 'That's expensive.'
topics.content_uz: Narx so'rash: 'How much is it?' 'I want to buy ___' deb ayting. Juda qimmat bo'lsa: 'That's expensive.'

topics.content: Numbers are everywhere: age, price, phone number. Say numbers one by one for phone numbers. Use 'years old' for age.
topics.content_uz: Raqamlar hamma joyda: yosh, narx, telefon raqami. Telefon raqamlarini birma-bir ayting. Yosh uchun 'years old' ishlating.

topics.content: To ask age: 'How old are you?' Answer: 'I am ___ years old.' For one year old, say 'I am one year old' (no 's' on year).
topics.content_uz: Yoshni so'rash: 'How old are you?' Javob: 'I am ___ years old.' Bir yosh uchun 'I am one year old' ('year'ga 's' qo'shilmaydi).

questions.question: Translate: 'U mening teng yoshimda'
questions.question_uz: Tarjimasi: 'He is the same age as me'
options: He is the same age as me
questions.answer: He is the same age as me

topics.content: Adjectives describe nouns. Put adjective before noun: 'big house', or after 'is': 'The house is big'.
topics.content_uz: Sifatlar otlarni tasvirlaydi. Sifatni ot oldidan qo'ying: 'big house', yoki 'is' dan keyin: 'The house is big'.

items.example: Hello, how are you? - I'm fine, thanks.
What is this? - It's a phone.
Where is the bathroom? - Over there.

topics.content: Simple conversation: Greeting → Question → Answer. Practice with a friend. Keep sentences short.
topics.content_uz: Oddiy suhbat: Salomlashish → Savol → Javob. Do'stingiz bilan mashq qiling. Gaplarni qisqa qiling.

questions.question: Complete dialog: A: 'What is this?' B: '___'

topics.content: Review: Greetings, family, colors, days, months, numbers 1-20, and simple verbs.
topics.content_uz: Takror: Salomlashish, oila, ranglar, kunlar, oylar, 1-20 raqamlari va oddiy fe'llar.

topics.content: Review: Time, jobs, likes/dislikes, classroom objects, weather, clothes, directions, shopping, age, adjectives.
topics.content_uz: Takror: Vaqt, kasblar, yoqtirish/yoqtirmaslik, sinf jihozlari, ob-havo, kiyimlar, yo'nalishlar, xarid qilish, yosh, sifatlar.
